title: always_comb is always called, even in case no change in sensitivity list
body:
Verilator 5.035 devel rev v5.034-123-ge4771dac7
Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS

Called this way:
`$(IVL_SVL) $(CELLS_SIM_SV) -binary --timing +1800-2023ext+sv --threads 8 --coverage --trace-fst --assert --top memCtrl_tb -I../memCtrl/sim -I../memCtrl/src memCtrl_tb.v memCtrl.sv`


In order to understand how `always_comb` works in SystemVerilog, I wrote an `always_ff` block which modifies some sensitives used in the `always_comb` block for five times. After that only the `always_ff` is expected to output text but not the `$display` used  in the  `always_comb` anymore because the `always_ff` block is no longer changing any  sensitivities used by  the `always_comb` block. However, it looks like verilator is calling `always_comb` anyway.
I checked against EDA Playground using a known commercial simulation which showed me the expected result.

I created a little module just doing the following:
Sourcefile ` memCtrl.sv`:

```
always_ff @(posedge i_clkRAM or negedge reset) 
    if (!reset) begin
        $display ("always_ff stateReset.");
        state<=stateReset;
      	i=0;
    end
    else begin
      if (next==delayAfterReset) begin
        $display ("always_ff state==delayAfterReset.");
        state<=delayAfterReset;
      end
      if (next==sendQPIWriteCmd) begin
        $display ("always_ff sendQPIWriteCmd.");
        if (i<5) i++;
      end
    end
  always_comb begin
    if (i==5) begin
      $display ("i ist 5.");
    end
    /* verilator lint_off CASEINCOMPLETE */
    case (state)
      stateReset: begin 
        $display ("always_comb stateReset.");
        next=delayAfterReset;
      end
      delayAfterReset: begin
        $display ("always_comb delayAfterReset.");
        next=sendQPIWriteCmd;
      end
      sendQPIWriteCmd: begin        
        $display ("always_comb sendQPIWriteCmd.");
      end
    endcase
  end

```
My testbench ist just doing the following:

```
initial begin
#1 _cs=1;
  reset=1;
  clkRAM = 1'b0;
  forever begin
    #1 clkRAM = ~clkRAM;  
  end
end

initial begin
#2        $display("Starting PSRAM write test bank 0. Resetting controller.");          
          reset=0;
          _cs=1; 
          bank=0;
#2        reset=1;
#2        
#100      ;

```
state is defined as  StateMachine:

```
typedef enum bit[10:0] {
  stateXXX,
  stateReset,
  delayAfterReset,
  enableClock,
  sendQPIEnable,
  stateIdle,
  sendQPIWriteCmd,
  sendQPIAddress,
  writeData,
  sendQPIReadCmd,
  readData,
  waitCycles
} StateMachine;

```

The output shows, that `always_comb` is called on each clock although when i==5, the sensitivity list will not be modified anymore.

So the output is:

`Starting PSRAM write test bank 0. Resetting controller.
always_ff stateReset.
always_comb stateReset.
always_ff state==delayAfterReset.
always_comb delayAfterReset.
always_ff sendQPIWriteCmd.
always_comb delayAfterReset.
always_ff sendQPIWriteCmd.
always_comb delayAfterReset.
always_ff sendQPIWriteCmd.
always_comb delayAfterReset.
always_ff sendQPIWriteCmd.
always_comb delayAfterReset.
always_ff sendQPIWriteCmd.
i ist 5.

Is there something I oversaw? Thanks.
